source_jobs.title: Sales Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

Davis Technology Management is a fast-growing technology services company providing:

• Managed IT Services
• Audio/Visual & Low Voltage Solutions
• Technology Staffing & Consulting
• Technology Product Development

We support restaurants, healthcare, and corporate clients across the U.S. and are entering a major growth phase.

We are building the operational and financial backbone to scale and this role is critical to that mission.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are hiring an Accounting & Operations Specialist to own the day-to-day financial and purchasing operations of the business.

This role sits at the intersection of:

• Accounting
• Purchasing & Procurement
• Billing & Revenue Operations
• Inventory & Project Margin Tracking

This is not just bookkeeping.

This is the operational engine that powers the business.


WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Accounting & Financial Operations

• Own QuickBooks Online
• Bank and credit card reconciliations
• Support month-end close
• Track revenue, COGS, and margins
• Support AP / AR

Billing & Revenue Workflow

• Ensure accurate and timely invoicing
• Track subscription and recurring billing
• Identify missed revenue or billing gaps

Purchasing & Procurement

• Manage vendors and distributors (Dell, Lenovo, Pax8, TD Synnex, etc.)
• Create and track purchase orders
• Track shipments and backorders

Inventory & Operational Workflow

• Maintain inventory accuracy
• Track equipment cost and margins
• Improve operational workflows


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

• 5+ years accounting, bookkeeping, or operations experience
• Strong QuickBooks Online experience
• AP / AR and reconciliations
• Vendor and purchase order experience
• Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills
• Highly detail oriented


HIGHLY PREFERRED (HUGE PLUS)

• MSP, VAR, IT Services, AV, Construction, or Professional Services experience

• Understanding of Microsoft licensing, SaaS software agreements, and financial commitments tied to licensing contracts and renewals

• Procurement or purchasing operations experience

• Subscription or recurring billing experience

• CRM tools such as ConnectWise or HubSpot


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Within 6-12 months, you will:

• Fully own QuickBooks and financial operations
• Improve billing accuracy and speed
• Create reliable purchasing workflows
• Provide visibility into margins and profitability
• Reduce operational workload for leadership


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role will help build the operational foundation of a rapidly growing technology company.

You will have real ownership, real impact, and help shape how

source_jobs.title: Junior Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 


POSITION: JUNIOR ANALYST-DEVELOPER




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are hiring a Junior Analyst-Developer to help improve business efficiency through automation and AI-assisted tools. This role combines process analysis with hands-on solution development using Microsoft’s automation ecosystem.

You will work directly with business teams to understand how their work gets done, identify inefficiencies, and create practical automation solutions. Success in this role comes from curiosity, logical thinking, and the ability to turn loosely defined problems into working solutions.

This position is ideal for someone early in their career who enjoys problem-solving and learning new technologies. Prior experience with specific automation tools is not required.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

• Meet with business users to understand workflows and identify improvement opportunities
• Design and build automation solutions using Microsoft’s automation and AI tools
• Create internal tools, workflows, and intelligent assistants to reduce manual work
• Document solutions and explain them clearly to end users
• Test, refine, and improve solutions based on feedback
• Support adoption of automation and AI tools across the organization


EXAMPLE PROJECTS

Examples of the types of problems you may solve include:

• Automatically processing incoming requests and routing them appropriately
• Creating workflows that track tasks and trigger reminders
• Building internal assistants that help teams find information faster
• Improving document and data handling processes


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field
• Strong problem-solving and logical thinking ability
• Comfortable communicating with both technical and non-technical individuals
• Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks
• Familiarity with Microsoft 365 tools such as Outlook, Teams, or SharePoint
• Interest in automation, AI, and process improvement


HELPFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED

• Exposure to automation tools, scripting, or workflow platforms
• Basic understanding of databases or data concepts


TRAINING & SUPPORT

You will receive hands-on training, mentorship, and real project experience. The goal is to quickly develop your ability to independently design and deliver automation solutions.


TOOLS & ENVIRONMENT

Microsoft automation and productivity platforms, cloud-based systems, and internal business applications.

source_jobs.title: Senior Service Desk Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

Davis Technology Management


POSITION OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Senior Service Desk Engineer to provide advanced technical support to enterprise clients and serve as a senior member of the service desk team.

This role acts as a technical escalation point, supports service delivery operations, and helps improve support processes. This position is also designed for someone ready to grow into a Lead or Service Delivery role.

The ideal candidate has strong troubleshooting skills, experience in enterprise environments, and understands how to operate within an SLA-driven support model.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


TECHNICAL SUPPORT

• Provide Tier II and Tier III support across multiple client environments
• Troubleshoot hardware, software, access, and system issues
• Support Windows, Microsoft 365, Active Directory / Entra ID, VPN, and endpoint management tools
• Manage user accounts, permissions, and device configurations
• Support remote and onsite users, including executives
• Resolve tickets within defined service levels
• Perform root cause analysis to prevent repeat issues




SERVICE DELIVERY & ITIL OPERATIONS

• Follow and support Incident, Request, and Escalation Management processes

• Ensure tickets are properly prioritized, documented, and resolved

• Assist in improving service desk workflows and procedures

• Contribute to documentation and knowledge base articles

• Identify recurring issues and recommend improvements

• Support change management activities when required

• Help maintain high service levels and client satisfaction




ESCALATIONS & OPERATIONAL SUPPORT

• Act as escalation point for complex or high-priority issues

• Assist with major incident response and resolution

• Work with infrastructure, engineering, and leadership teams

• Help maintain stability and continuity across client environments




TEAM & LEADERSHIP SUPPORT

• Mentor junior service desk engineers

• Assist with onboarding and training

• Help reinforce service standards and processes

• Support leadership with escalations and workload management

• Be ready to step into a Lead role as needed


CLIENT SUPPORT

• Provide professional, responsive support

• Communicate clearly with technical and non-technical users

• Build trust with clients and end users

• Support a high-quality service experience




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• 7+ years of IT support or service desk experience

• Experience supporting enterprise environments

• Strong troubleshooting skills across endpoints, identity, and cloud platforms

• Experience working in SLA-driven support environments

• Strong communication and customer service skills

• Ability to work independently and take ownership




SERVICE DELIVERY & ITIL EXPERIENCE (PREFERRED)

• Experience working in ITIL-based environments

• Understanding of:

– Incident Management
– Request Management
– Escalation Management
– Change Management

• Experience in managed services environments preferred

• Experience improving processes or documentation preferred




PREFERRED CERTIFICATIONS

• ITIL Foundation (preferred)

• Microsoft Certifications

• CompTIA A+, Network+, or Security+


KEY COMPETENCIES

• Advanced troubleshooting

• Service delivery mindset

• ITIL awareness

• Leadership potential

• Strong communication

• Ownership and accountability




CAREER PATH

This role can grow into:

• Service

source_jobs.title: Workday Technical Consultant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

We are looking for a technical consultant with strong experience supporting and enhancing enterprise HR and financial systems within the Workday ecosystem. This individual will play a key role in improving system functionality, supporting integrations, and partnering with business stakeholders to deliver scalable technical solutions across multiple Workday modules.

This role will work closely with internal teams including HR, Payroll, Finance, and IT to help maintain system stability, optimize workflows, and support ongoing platform enhancements. The ideal candidate should be comfortable balancing technical development, troubleshooting, reporting, and business-facing collaboration in a fast-paced environment.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Support and enhance Workday configurations, workflows, and overall system functionality

 - Develop and maintain integrations between Workday and third-party systems

 - Assist with reporting, analytics, dashboards, and data validation efforts

 - Partner with functional and technical stakeholders to gather requirements and recommend scalable solutions

 - Investigate and resolve issues tied to integrations, reporting logic, system performance, or data accuracy

 - Participate in testing cycles, deployments, system upgrades, and production support activities

 - Contribute to ongoing process improvements and platform optimization initiatives


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong technical understanding of the Workday platform and its core architecture

 - Experience working with integrations, reporting, and system configuration in a production environment

 - Ability to troubleshoot complex technical issues and communicate solutions clearly to both technical and non-technical teams

 - Experience supporting enterprise business applications in a collaborative environment

 - Familiarity with HR, Payroll, Financials, or Time Tracking processes within Workday


ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE THAT WOULD BE HELPFUL

 - Exposure to advanced reporting or analytics capabilities

 - Experience supporting custom applications or platform extensions

 - Understanding of Workday security concepts and reporting tools

 - Background working in environments with ongoing enhancements, integrations, or digital transformation initiatives

This position is ideal for someone who is hands-on technically, enjoys solving problems, and can work cross-functionally to improve business processes and overall system performance. We can only accept US citizens and green card holders for this position. Please submit your Linked In profile too.

source_jobs.title: Field Technician Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

Location: DFW
Employment Type: Full-Time


OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Field Technician Lead with strong hands-on experience in Audio Visual systems, low voltage cabling, and customer support. This individual will lead field installations, guide junior technicians, and ensure projects are completed safely, on time, and to a high standard.

The ideal candidate is both technically skilled and customer-focused, capable of troubleshooting complex systems while maintaining professional communication with clients.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


FIELD INSTALLATION & TECHNICAL WORK

 - Install, configure, and support Audio Visual systems including displays, projectors, conferencing equipment, speakers, and control systems

 - Perform low voltage cabling installation including Cat6, fiber, coax, speaker wire, and control wiring

 - Terminate, test, and certify network and AV cabling

 - Mount displays, install equipment racks, and integrate AV hardware

 - Troubleshoot AV, networking, and signal flow issues in the field


TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Lead and coordinate field technicians on installation projects

 - Provide guidance and mentorship to junior technicians

 - Ensure projects follow installation standards, safety requirements, and quality expectations

 - Coordinate daily field work, materials, and project timelines


CUSTOMER SUPPORT

 - Serve as the primary on-site contact for clients

 - Clearly communicate installation progress and technical issues

 - Provide post-installation walkthroughs and basic system training for customers

 - Deliver professional service that creates a strong customer experience


PROJECT COORDINATION

 - Work with project managers and operations teams to ensure smooth installations

 - Review project plans, diagrams, and scopes before installation

 - Identify potential issues early and escalate when necessary

 - Document installations, updates, and service work


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - 5+ years of experience in field technician roles

 - Hands-on experience with Audio Visual systems installation

 - Experience installing low voltage cabling (Cat5/6, fiber, coax, speaker wire)

 - Strong troubleshooting skills across AV and basic networking systems

 - Experience leading or mentoring technicians on job sites

 - Ability to read project drawings, wiring diagrams, and installation plans


TECHNICAL SKILLS

 - AV display installation and calibration

 - Conference room systems and video conferencing equipment

 - Equipment rack building and cable management

 - Network cable termination and testing

 - Signal flow troubleshooting

 - Basic networking knowledge


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - AVIXA / CTS certification

 - Low Voltage or Structured Cabling certifications

 - Experience with Crestron, Extron, Q-SYS, or similar control systems

 - OSHA or job-site safety training


KEY TRAITS

 - Strong leadership and accountability

 - Detail-oriented and organized

 - Professional communication with customers

 - Ability to work independently in the field

 - Problem-solving mindsetField Technician Lead (Audio Visual & Low Voltage)

source_jobs.title: IT Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

We are seeking a mid-level IT Project Manager to oversee and coordinate delivery across a portfolio of 6–8 concurrent technology initiatives, including AI enablement, intelligent automation, data analytics, application modernization, and cloud infrastructure. This role is responsible for maintaining a consolidated view of all initiatives, ensuring teams and vendors are accountable to timelines and quality standards, and proactively identifying risks and dependencies.

The Project Manager will establish consistent reporting cadences, provide clear executive-level updates, and ensure alignment between technical teams, business stakeholders, and leadership. The ideal candidate is highly organized, delivery-focused, and comfortable managing multiple workstreams simultaneously while driving outcomes, improving visibility, and bringing structure to a fast-paced environment.

Portfolio Coordination & Delivery Management

 - Maintain a consolidated view of all active initiatives — status, milestones, owners, dependencies, and risks

 - Hold internal teams and vendor partners accountable for committed deliverables and timelines

 - Identify and manage cross-initiative dependencies and resource conflicts

 - Verify that deliverables meet acceptance criteria — not just "done" but "done right"

Communication & Stakeholder Management

 - Establish and maintain a regular reporting cadence (weekly team updates, biweekly executive summaries)

 - Prepare clear, concise portfolio status updates for the Director of Architecture and CIO

 - Proactively identify communication gaps between teams, between business and IT, and between onshore and offshore contributors

 - Tailor communications to the audience — technical detail for delivery teams, outcome-focused summaries for executives

Risk, Issue & Vendor Management

 - Proactively identify delivery risks and escalate early with recommended mitigations

 - Track and manage issue resolution across initiatives

 - Coordinate with vendor partners on delivery expectations, SOW commitments, and quality standards. A small portion of the automation initiatives involve an offshore vendor team

 - Ensure vendor deliveries are aligned with timelines and integrated with internal workstreams

Active Initiative Portfolio

The PM will coordinate across the following initiative areas:

 - AI Enablement: Enterprise Microsoft Copilot adoption (M365 Copilot, PowerBI Copilot, Copilot Studio), AI for SDLC (developer tooling and GenAI frameworks)

 - Intelligent Automations: Agentic automations with Copilot Studio, Power Platform migration from legacy RPA

 - Data & Analytics: Data analytics platform build-out (pipeline modernization, semantic models, self-service reporting)

 - Application Modernization: Containerization (ECS migration from EC2-hosted workloads)

 - Infrastructure: Cloud infrastructure optimization initiatives

 - Management Reporting: Executive dashboards, initiative scorecards, and periodic leadership updates

Must-Have Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, Computer Science, or a related discipline

 - Experience managing multiple concurrent IT initiatives simultaneously — portfolio-level coordination, not just single-project delivery

 - Strong accountability and escalation discipline — comfortable holding people to commitments and raising flag

source_jobs.title: Software Architect
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

We are seeking a senior-level Software Architect specializing in Data & Analytics to lead the design and implementation of a modern enterprise analytics platform. This individual will define the organization’s target-state data architecture, establish scalable data pipelines and reporting frameworks, and create the foundational standards that enable reliable, self-service analytics across the business.

This is a strategic and hands-on role responsible for simplifying a complex data landscape, optimizing tooling and costs, and building a scalable Lakehouse-style architecture supporting both operational and analytical reporting. The architect will work closely with engineering teams and business stakeholders to ensure data is structured, governed, and delivered in a way that supports meaningful decision-making.

In addition to architecture leadership, the role includes hands-on prototyping, defining governance and data quality standards, mentoring teams, and guiding the organization toward a mature, scalable, and cost-effective analytics capability.

Key Focus Areas

Architecture & Strategy

• Define enterprise data architecture, pipelines, and reporting layers
• Establish scalable Lakehouse / medallion data models (bronze, silver, gold)
• Simplify and rationalize existing analytics tools and platforms
• Develop long-term roadmap aligned with business goals

Hands-On Technical Leadership

• Build reference implementations and architectural blueprints
• Prototype solutions and guide development teams
• Ensure scalability, performance, and maintainability

Governance & Standards

• Implement data governance, quality, and security frameworks
• Establish cataloging, naming conventions, and documentation standards
• Enable self-service analytics while maintaining data integrity

Stakeholder Engagement & Enablement

• Partner with business leaders to support strategic reporting needs
• Mentor engineering teams on architecture best practices
• Translate complex technical concepts into clear business value

Must-Have

• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline
• 7–10+ years of experience in software architecture with strong focus on enterprise data and analytics platforms
• Proven experience designing and implementing modern data architectures, including ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and Lakehouse / medallion patterns
• Strong data modeling expertise across both operational (normalized) and analytical (dimensional / star schema) models
• Experience building semantic layers and enabling self-service reporting, preferably with Power BI
• Hands-on experience with enterprise data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, AWS data services, or equivalent
• Experience implementing data governance frameworks, including data quality, lineage, cataloging, and stewardship
• Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical teams and business stakeholders
• Strategic mindset with the ability to design scalable, long-term analytics solutions aligned to business decision-making


NICE-TO-HAVE

• Cloud or data certifications (AWS Solutions Architect, AWS Data Analytics Specialty, Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Engineer, etc.)
• Experience working within AWS or Microsoft Fabric environments
• Familiarity with Power BI Copilot or natural language analytics capabilities
• Experience supporting lo

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 


ABOUT DAVIS TECHNOLOGY MANAGEMENT

Davis Technology Management partners with organizations across the U.S. to design, build, and support modern technology solutions. Our services span Technology Consulting, IT Staffing, and Managed Services, supporting clients at critical points in their growth.

Our mission is simple: create RAVING FANS through exceptional execution, accountability, and results.

We hire engineers who take ownership, solve real problems, and elevate the teams around them.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are looking for a Senior Full-Stack Engineer who has spent years building real-world applications and understands what it takes to design, deliver, and support systems that people depend on.

This is not a narrow role.

You will be trusted to own meaningful technical responsibility for designing solutions, building them, improving them, and supporting them in live environments.

You’ll work on modern applications built in cloud-native environments where performance, scalability, and reliability matter.

This role is ideal for engineers who:

• Take pride in what they build
• Have operated in production environments
• Understand the full lifecycle of applications
• Are comfortable being counted on




WHAT YOU’LL ACTUALLY DO

• Build and improve applications used in real business environments

• Work across both front-end and back-end components

• Design and support applications running in AWS-based environments

• Own problems through resolution, not just implementation

• Improve existing systems and help design new ones

• Troubleshoot production issues and implement long-term fixes

• Collaborate with other engineers, leadership, and stakeholders

• Help set the technical standard for quality and execution




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM SENIOR ENGINEERS

We are looking for engineers who have developed strong instincts from experience.

This includes people who:

• Have spent 7+ years building and supporting production applications

• Have significant hands-on experience working in AWS environments

• Understand how applications behave beyond local development

• Have worked on systems that required reliability and performance

• Can independently work through complex technical problems

• Have supported or mentored other developers

• Take ownership and accountability seriously




THIS ROLE IS LIKELY A STRONG FIT IF YOU’VE…

• Built and supported applications that others rely on daily

• Been responsible for deploying and maintaining production systems

• Worked directly in cloud environments beyond basic exposure

• Been trusted with meaningful technical responsibility

• Helped improve systems, not just contribute code




THIS ROLE IS LIKELY NOT A FIT IF YOU…

• Have primarily worked in training, academic, or isolated environments

• Have limited exposure to real production systems

• Prefer narrowly defined or highly structured tasks

• Are uncomfortable operating with ownership and autonomy




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE HERE

Successful engineers at Davis Technology Management:

• Take ownership

• Solve problems proactively

• Elevate the team around them

• Deliver reliable, scalable solutions

• Continuously improve systems and processes




WHY ENGINEERS JOIN DTM

• Work on meaningful, real-world systems

• High level of trust and autonomy

• Opportunity to influence technical direction

• Growth into technica

source_jobs.title: Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Davis Technology Management is a successful technology company in the IT staffing, Technology Consulting and IT Managed Services sector. Our focus is crafting technical and staffing solutions that empower businesses and foster growth. Our foundation is built on a commitment to our Core Values, and a team dedicated to bridging the gap between innovative IT solutions and businesses seeking to navigate the complexities of the digital age.

 

At the heart of everything we do, we’re dedicated to exceeding expectations and creating RAVING FANS both within our internal team and amongst our clients.

 

This role is primarily office-based due to the hands-on hardware support required, with up to 25% travel to customer sites across the U.S. The position is highly customer-focused and involves onboarding new customers, configuring sensor hardware and gateways, setting up monitoring dashboards, and ensuring successful deployments. You'll work closely with customers and the support team to provide ongoing support and resolve issues. If bugs are identified, you'll document findings, create tickets for the development team, and assist with troubleshooting and testing solutions



What We're Looking For

• Experience supporting connected hardware and software environments, including troubleshooting communication and connectivity issues between devices and systems

• Ability to work within Linux-based environments and leverage scripting or automation tools to streamline deployments, diagnostics, and system maintenance

• Familiarity with modern application deployment and containerization practices in production environments

• Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills, with the ability to identify root causes across hardware, software, networking, and cloud-based systems

• Experience supporting data-driven applications and investigating system anomalies through log analysis, telemetry data, and operational reporting

• Ability to document findings clearly, communicate technical issues effectively, and collaborate with engineering teams to drive issue resolution

• Exposure to cloud-based architectures used for device management, data ingestion, event processing, and system monitoring

• Comfortable working directly with customers and internal teams to configure, deploy, maintain, and optimize technical solutions

• Hands-on experience diagnosing and resolving system faults, network communication issues, and hardware/software configuration challenges

• Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining a high level of accuracy, organization, and customer service

notes: IT staffing and consulting company with a mix of technical and non-technical roles
